column5: Distinguish between rational and irrational numbers.
Approximate irrational numbers and locate them on a number line.
Apply properties of integer exponents.
Use square root and cube root symbols.
Use scientific notation to estimate large/small quantities.
Perform operations with scientific notation.
Interpret expressions and parts of an expression.
Describe and solve linear equations in one variable.
Create and solve linear equations from real-world contexts.
Solve systems of linear equations algebraically.
Solve real-world problems using systems of linear equations.
Understand that a function is a rule assigning one output to each input.
Compare properties of two functions represented differently.
Interpret the equation y = mx + b as a linear function.
Verify properties of rotations, reflections, and translations.
Understand congruence through physical models or software.
Describe the effect of dilations, translations, rotations, and reflections on 2D figures.
Explain a proof of the Pythagorean Theorem.
Apply the Pythagorean Theorem in real-world problems.
Apply the Pythagorean Theorem to find the distance between two points.
Construct and interpret scatter plots.
Know that straight lines are used to model relationships.
Use the equation of a linear model to solve problems.
